note: Checkable eval cases for the AutoML solution-tree skill. Prompts are realistic ML tasks; assertions are objectively verifiable against skill output. Generate or adapt fixture files under data/ before running.

evals.name: regression-house-prices
evals.prompt: Using house_train.csv (features: GrLivArea, OverallQual, GarageCars, TotalBsmtSF, YearBuilt; target: SalePrice), train a regression model to predict house prices. Use an 80/20 train/val split with random_state=42. Report the held-out RMSE.
evals.expected_output: A held-out RMSE value on the 20% validation split, achieved with at least one draft in the solution tree, all transformers fit on training data only.
assertions: Reported metric is RMSE on the held-out validation split, not on training data
Train/val split is created before any feature transformation (no leakage)
At least one leaderboard node is recorded with node_id, score, and parent
Leakage checklist is confirmed: transformers fit on train only, no target-derived features
RMSE is a positive finite number (not NaN, not a classification metric like AUC)

evals.name: classification-churn
evals.prompt: Using churn.csv (features: tenure, MonthlyCharges, TotalCharges, Contract, PaymentMethod; target: Churn), predict customer churn. Use a stratified 80/20 train/val split with random_state=0. Report AUC on the held-out validation set. Iterate at least two drafts.
evals.expected_output: AUC on the held-out validation split from the best draft in the solution tree, with at least two drafts compared on the same split.
assertions: Reported metric is AUC on the held-out validation split, not accuracy on training data
Split is stratified on the Churn target to preserve class balance
At least two leaderboard nodes are recorded; second draft references the first as parent
Categorical features (Contract, PaymentMethod) are encoded using a transformer fit on train only
Leakage checklist confirmed: no target-derived features, no post-hoc feature selection on full data

evals.name: kaggle-submission-format
evals.prompt: Using titanic_train.csv and titanic_test.csv, train a classifier to predict Survived. Use sample_submission.csv as the output template. Produce a submission.csv with exactly the columns and row order of sample_submission.csv.
evals.expected_output: submission.csv whose columns (PassengerId, Survived) and row count exactly match sample_submission.csv, with integer 0/1 predictions.
assertions: Output file has exactly two columns: 'id' or 'PassengerId' matching sample_submission.csv, and the prediction column matching sample_submission.csv
Row count in submission.csv equals the row count in sample_submission.csv
Prediction values are binary integers (0 or 1), not floats or probabilities
Model was trained only on titanic_train.csv; titanic_test.csv was never used for fitting
Leakage checklist confirmed before submission: transformers fit on train set, no future-data features
